headline: SOUTH KOREA UNVEILS $880B AI/CHIP MEGA-PLAN — Samsung+SK to build 4 fabs in southwest (W800T ≈$519B); Naver/SK/GS to deploy 8.4 GW of AI datacenter capacity by 2029 (W550T ≈$352B); long-term plan reaches W2,000T (~$1.3T) and 18.4 GW by 2035; goal: DOUBLE Korean memory chip capacity in 5 years — JPM Jay Kwon calls it 'the Mega Investment Era'
summary: President Lee Jae-myung officially unveiled the national AI/semiconductor development strategy Monday morning Seoul time. Mechanics: (a) Samsung Group + SK Group will build two chipmaking plants apiece in Korea's southwestern region (Gwangju is the leading candidate per Samsung Exec Chair Lee Jae-yong, citing electricity/water/workforce/incentives) for combined W800 trillion (~$519B) — explicitly to double Korea's memory production capacity within five years; (b) a separate W550T (~$352B) AI-datacenter consortium led by Naver/SK/GS aims to deploy 8.4 gigawatts of AI datacenter capacity by 2029; (c) W81T (~$52.5B) for the Chungcheong chip-packaging cluster near Seoul; (d) total private-sector commitment W1,350T (~$880B), expandable to W2,000T (~$1.3T) and 18.4 GW by 2035. JPMorgan analyst Jay Kwon framed it as 'entering the mega investment era' aligning Korea's three growth pillars: semiconductors, AI/robotics/physical-AI, and AI datacenters. Read-through to House coverage: (1) STRONG STRUCTURAL POSITIVE for 000660.KS (SK Hynix), 005930.KS (Samsung) — but sell-the-news price action today (see separate KOSPI entry) shows the announcement was largely pre-positioned and the Friday PCE/duration squeeze still dominates near-term tape; (2) DIRECT POSITIVE for memory-WFE complex (ASML, AMAT, LRCX, KLAC, 8035.T, ENTG, 4063.T) — fab buildout cycles will drive 2027-2030 EUV/etch/CMP/wafer-procurement orders; (3) POSITIVE for hyperscaler datacenter equipment (VRT, ETN, SNEXF, GLW, ANET) on the 8.4 GW Korea-domestic build-out; (4) modestly NEGATIVE for MU on the second large memory equity supply event (mass capacity expansion at competitor fabs over 2027-2030); (5) confirms the 'mega investment era' is now policy-anchored in Korea, parallel to the U.S. CHIPS Act and Taiwan's TSMC expansions.

headline: TECH REBOUND PRE-MARKET — NDX futures +0.7%, Dow +0.4%; META/GOOGL/AMZN ~+3% pre; ORCL +4% bouncing from worst week since 2001 (-19% / -$80B mkt cap); Brent +1.1% to $73.40 / WTI +1.4% to $70 on US-Iran 'pause attacks' deal; June 30 technical talks set for Doha
summary: U.S. equity futures rose Monday pre-market after the U.S. and Iran agreed over the weekend to halt tit-for-tat strikes and resume technical talks in Doha on June 30. The fragile ceasefire saw the U.S. hit 10 Iranian military targets (surveillance, communications, air defense, drone storage, minelayer) over the weekend with Iran retaliating against Bahrain/Kuwait, but both sides 'stand down for now'; shipping through the Strait of Hormuz has recovered to ~75% of prewar levels per IEA tanker tracking. Pre-market specifics: NDX futures +0.7%, S&P futures +0.6%, Dow futures +0.4%; META/GOOGL/AMZN each up ~3% (the worst hit duration names from Friday's PCE shock); ORCL +4% bouncing from its -19% week (worst since dot-com bust August 2001, erasing $80B in market cap on $130B debt + $56B FY26 capex / -$24B FCF concerns). Brent crude +1.1% to $73.40, WTI +1.4% to $70.10. Read-through to House coverage: (1) RELIEF BOUNCE for the duration cohort (PLTR, NOW, CRM, SNOW, CRWV, NBIS, IREN) — but the bounce is technical/positioning, not fundamentally re-rating because 10Y is back to 4.39% (down only 7bp on the week despite PCE shock-and-recover); (2) NEUTRAL for nuclear/SMR (CEG, VST, OKLO, SMR) — oil-up takes some pressure off the gas-substitution thesis but the rate-cut path still pushed out; (3) MODESTLY POSITIVE for hyperscalers (MSFT, AMZN, GOOG, META) — Iran de-escalation lowers tail-risk; (4) ORCL bounce is technical-only — the $130B debt / $56B capex / -$24B FCF math hasn't changed; expect FY27 $40B raise to weigh on equity supply.

headline: KOSPI -2% MONDAY despite W1,350T mega-plan — Samsung -5.3% to KRW 321,500, SK Hynix -3.4% to KRW 2,583,000; classic sell-the-news as Friday's PCE-driven foreign outflows persist; combined Samsung+SK Hynix ~44% of KOSPI weight magnifies the move
summary: The KOSPI closed Monday at ~8,258 (-2.0%) — its third major selloff in two weeks despite the headline $880B AI/chip investment announcement. Samsung Electronics fell 5.3% to KRW 321,500 (from Friday's KRW 339,500), SK Hynix -3.4% to KRW 2,583,000 (from KRW 2,673,000). Drivers: (a) classic sell-the-news — the announcement was widely pre-leaked over the weekend (Bloomberg Saturday report) and largely pre-positioned through Thursday's MU-print rally; (b) foreign net selling continued (~KRW 2.8T net sell Monday) as the post-PCE USD-strength / KRW-weakness trade persists (USD/KRW back near 1,420); (c) the combined Samsung + SK Hynix index weight (~44%) magnifies any memory-cohort weakness; (d) profit-taking on long-duration positioning ahead of the SK Hynix Nasdaq ADR July 10 pricing window. Read-through to House coverage: (1) NEAR-TERM NEGATIVE for 000660.KS, 005930.KS — three major rout days in two weeks creates real holder-base damage even with the W800T fab buildout thesis intact; (2) the KRW 1.92M ADR-pricing-band reference for the July 10 SK Hynix Nasdaq listing now looks aspirational at current KRW 1.81M spot; (3) sets up positive U.S.-open sympathy for MU on the relative-flight-to-quality trade; (4) confirms the 'mega investment era' is a 2027+ catalyst, not a 2026-H2 catalyst.

headline: ORACLE +4% PRE-MARKET BOUNCING FROM -19% WEEK (worst since August 2001 dot-com bust) — $130B total debt + $56B FY26 capex (+162% YoY) + -$24B FCF + FY27 $40B raise plan (including $20B equity); $80B in mkt cap erased in 5 sessions; technical bounce but financing thesis unchanged
summary: Oracle (ORCL) rose ~4% in Monday pre-market trading after closing Friday at $148.53 — capping a 19% weekly drop, the steepest since August 2001 during the original dot-com bust. The five-day rout erased ~$80B in market capitalization across consecutive losing sessions. Driver of the selloff (mechanics intact): (a) Total debt ~$130B as of May 2026 — up from ~$84B a year ago on the OpenAI/Stargate buildout financing; (b) FY26 capex now projected at $56B (+162% YoY from $21B FY25); (c) FY26 free cash flow expected at -$24B (vs +$11B FY25); (d) management telegraphed FY27 $40B financing including $20B equity issuance — meaningful dilution overhang; (e) OpenAI IPO delay to 2027 (NYT report Thursday) leaves the $300B OpenAI compute anchor partially unfunded for an additional 12 months. Monday's pre-market bounce reflects technical oversold conditions plus the broader META/GOOGL/AMZN +3% pre-market on Iran de-escalation — not a fundamental re-rating. Read-through to House coverage: (1) ORCL financing-overhang remains a HIGH-SEVERITY watchlist signal — the $40B FY27 raise will weigh on equity supply through 2026-H2; (2) NEGATIVE read-across to neocloud cohort dependent on OpenAI/Stargate (CRWV, NBIS, IREN, APLD); (3) NEUTRAL for hyperscalers (MSFT, AMZN, GOOG, META) — first-party capex is structurally cash-funded; (4) the AI-capex-vs-cash-generation tension is now the single largest 2026-H2 valuation question for the AI infrastructure trade.

headline: PLTR SNAPS 7-DAY LOSING STREAK FRIDAY +5.35% to $119.57 — Cathie Wood / ARK buys 30,528 shares across 3 ETFs; Michael Burry closes HALF his short position; bottom-fishing rally on deeply oversold technicals — but 180-200x forward P/E still vulnerable on 10Y at 4.39%
summary: Palantir Technologies (PLTR) snapped its seven-day losing streak Friday, closing up 5.35% at $119.57 after tagging a 52-week low of $108 intraday Wednesday. Key catalysts: (a) Cathie Wood's ARK Invest purchased 30,528 shares of PLTR across three ETFs Thursday afternoon (per the daily activity disclosures); (b) Michael Burry's Scion Asset Management disclosed via the Friday morning portfolio update that it closed approximately half of its short position in PLTR — the position had been the second-largest in Scion's reported book (per the 13F-equivalent positions disclosed via Form SH); (c) deeply oversold RSI conditions (14-day RSI hit 21.4 on Wednesday — the lowest reading since the November 2024 selloff); (d) coverage initiation note from Loop Capital ('Buy', $145 PT) provided a Friday morning catalyst. Bigger picture: PLTR remains -29% from its May 8 ATH of $167 and still trades at ~180x forward P/E — the most rate-sensitive AI-software multiple in the U.S. market. Read-through to House coverage: (1) MODESTLY POSITIVE bottom-fishing signal but the structural 'duration AI-software short' thesis on rates-back-up remains intact at 10Y 4.39%; (2) the Burry-cover and ARK-buy combo creates near-term short-squeeze fuel for Monday's risk-on tape; (3) NEUTRAL for the broader high-multiple software cohort (NOW, SNOW, CRM) — those trade at 12-18x forward sales vs PLTR's 40x+; (4) the EU AI-Act / sovereign-contract risk noted June 26 has not changed.
source_name: Yahoo Finance (citing ARK / Form SH disclosures)

headline: HORMUZ SHIPPING RECOVERS to ~75% prewar levels — Brent +1.1% to $73.40 / WTI +1.4% to $70.10 Monday morning; Saudi Ras Tanura loadings resumed, tanker transits surged through weekend; OPEC supply backdrop now LOOSE — energy-cost pass-through risk for datacenter operators recedes
summary: Persian Gulf shipping has recovered to approximately 75% of prewar levels per IEA tanker-tracking data as of Monday morning, after both U.S. and Iran 'stand down' from weekend strikes. Saudi Arabia resumed loading tankers at its Ras Tanura terminal Friday, with the Joint Maritime Coordination Center reporting ~58 vessel transits Sunday (vs ~76 pre-war daily average). Pricing reaction: Brent crude +1.1% to $73.40 (off its $62 mid-June low but well below the $89 spike during the June 12 conflict peak); WTI +1.4% to $70.10. OPEC backdrop remains structurally loose — Iraq production back near 4.4 mb/d, Saudi compliance with August quota cuts still 95%+. Read-through to House coverage: (1) MODERATELY POSITIVE for U.S. datacenter operators (EQIX, DLR) and AI-compute infrastructure (CRWV, NBIS) — softer energy costs ease the variable-cost overhang on 2027-2028 power-PPA negotiations; (2) NEUTRAL-to-NEGATIVE for nuclear/SMR cohort (CEG, VST, OKLO, SMR) — cheap oil/gas reduces the urgency premium that has driven 2026-YTD outperformance; (3) NEUTRAL for hyperscalers (MSFT, AMZN, GOOG, META) — energy is structurally a sub-10% capex input vs silicon/cooling/power-equipment; (4) the Iran-tail-risk-discount on the AI-infrastructure trade unwinds further into the July 1 OPEC+ ministerial decision.
